1.anchor: This is what happened during the time of Xerxes,
the Xerxes who ruled over 127 provinces stretching from India to Cush
1.note: That is, the upper Nile region

6.anchor: who had been carried into exile from Jerusalem by Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, among those taken captive with Jehoiachin

21.anchor: During the time Mordecai was sitting at the king’s gate, Bigthana

7.anchor: (that is, the lot) was cast in the presence of Haman to select a day and month. And the lot fell on

9.anchor: If it pleases the king, let a decree be issued to destroy them, and I will give ten thousand talents
9.note: That is, about 375 tons or about 340 metric tons

14.anchor: His wife Zeresh and all his friends said to him, “Have a pole set up, reaching to a height of fifty cubits,
14.note: That is, about 75 feet or about 23 meters

25.anchor: But when the plot came to the king’s attention,
25.note: Or when Esther came before the king

4.anchor: For I and my people have been sold to be destroyed, killed and annihilated. If we had merely been sold as male and female slaves, I would have kept quiet, because no such distress would justify disturbing the king.
4.note: Or quiet, but the compensation our adversary offers cannot be compared with the loss the king would suffer

9.anchor: Then Harbona, one of the eunuchs attending the king, said, “A pole reaching to a height of fifty cubits
9.note: That is, about 75 feet or about 23 meters

9.anchor: At once the royal secretaries were summoned—on the twenty-third day of the third month, the month of Sivan. They wrote out all Mordecai’s orders to the Jews, and to the satraps, governors and nobles of the 127 provinces stretching from India to Cush.
9.note: That is, the upper Nile region

11.anchor: The king’s edict granted the Jews in every city the right to assemble and protect themselves; to destroy, kill and annihilate the armed men of any nationality or province who might attack them and their women and children,
11.note: Or province, together with their women and children, who might attack them;
